summary: Matched Dolphin OS __OSUnhandledException in C by removing the MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementation and its separate data assembly file, folding report strings and exception switch behavior into src/dolphin/os/OSError.c. The PR also moved the __DIRegs hardware register declaration from OSInit.c into OSInit.h so OSError.c could reference it, and removed asm/dolphin/os/OSError_data.s from obj_files.mk.
what_changed: Deleted asm/dolphin/os/OSError_data.s, which previously held MUST_MATCH-only report string data, the __OSUnhandledException jump table jtbl_804022D4, and sdata label lbl_804D5C18.
Removed the OSError_data.s object from TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk.
Removed the inline assembly version of __OSUnhandledException from OSError.c and kept a C implementation that now matches closely enough to replace it.

Adjusted __OSUnhandledException formatting strings to match the original data exactly, including changing "DSISR= 0x%08x" to "DSISR = 0x%08x" and adding the explicit "TB = 0x%016llx\n" OSReport call using OSGetTime().
Changed the error 15 hardware register reporting from raw pointer casts at 0xCC005030, 0xCC005032, 0xCC005000+0x20/+0x22, and 0xCC006014 to named register arrays __DSPRegs and __DIRegs.
Fixed the final last-interrupt report call to pass __OSLastInterruptTime for the %016llx field in addition to __OSLastInterrupt and __OSLastInterruptSrr0.
Added includes for <dolphin/dsp/dsp.h> and <dolphin/os/OSInit.h> in OSError.c to expose __DSPRegs and __DIRegs.
Moved the __DIRegs declaration out of OSInit.c and into OSInit.h as extern vu32 __DIRegs[0x10] AT_ADDRESS(0xCC006000), making the DI hardware register block reusable by OSError.c.
smart_moves: Converted anonymous absolute-address loads into named hardware register arrays, which made the C expression cleaner and likely helped produce the expected addressing sequence while improving maintainability.
Promoted __DIRegs to a header instead of duplicating an extern declaration locally in OSError.c, establishing a shared declaration point for the DI register block.
Matched string data by using literal OSReport format strings in C, allowing the separate OSError_data.s file and hand-maintained labels to be deleted.
Used OSReport("TB = 0x%016llx\n", OSGetTime()) directly, matching the original 64-bit time print behavior and avoiding the prior placeholder OSReport("", time, (u32) time) workaround.
Removed the MUST_MATCH asm branch entirely once the C implementation matched, reducing future maintenance burden and making __OSUnhandledException easier to inspect.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin OS exception/reporting functions, exact format-string spelling, spacing, and newline placement matter because strings can drive data layout and matching.
If an asm data file only exists to host report strings or jump tables for an inline asm fallback, a successful C match can often eliminate both the asm function and the data object.
Hardware register accesses should prefer existing named volatile register arrays such as __DSPRegs and __DIRegs over raw pointer casts when that produces correct code and clearer intent.
Shared memory-mapped register declarations belong in headers when multiple C files need them; this avoids scattered local externs and keeps AT_ADDRESS declarations consistent.
When reproducing varargs calls like OSReport, ensure every format specifier has a matching argument, especially 64-bit %llx fields such as OSGetTime() and __OSLastInterruptTime.
Switch cases that originally compiled through an assembly jump table can still be represented naturally in C when the case values and report calls are arranged to reproduce the same behavior.
naming_conventions: Preserved SDK-style double-underscore internal names such as __OSUnhandledException, __OSErrorTable, __OSLastInterrupt, __OSLastInterruptSrr0, __OSLastInterruptTime, __DSPRegs, and __DIRegs.
Used OS_ERROR_DSI and other OS error enum-style constants where available, while retaining raw case 15 where the excerpt does not show a named constant.
Moved away from synthetic labels such as lbl_80401FF8, lbl_804D5C18, and jtbl_804022D4 once equivalent C literals/control flow matched.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted a large inline asm implementation guarded by MUST_MATCH after replacing it with equivalent C.
Eliminated separate assembly data labels by relying on C string literals and switch lowering.
Matched load behavior for AI/ARAM/DI DMA address reporting through indexed volatile register arrays: __DSPRegs[24], __DSPRegs[25], __DSPRegs[16], __DSPRegs[17], and __DIRegs[5].
Used a direct 64-bit OSGetTime() result in a varargs OSReport call for the TB field.

Preserved exception handler dispatch semantics: call registered __OSErrorTable[error] handler with scheduler disabled, re-enable scheduler, reschedule, then OSLoadContext; special-case error 8 by loading context before reporting unhandled exception.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one review, but the provided excerpt contains no review body or actionable reviewer feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find other Dolphin OS files that still use MUST_MATCH inline asm with separate *_data.s files for strings or jump tables.
Search for raw 0xCC00xxxx hardware register pointer casts that could be replaced by named volatile register arrays.
Audit OSReport calls with %llx format strings for missing or incorrectly split 64-bit arguments.
Check whether error case 15 has a named OS_ERROR_* constant that could replace the raw case value without affecting matching.
